{{Quote|This tactic came in handy when we made an assault on a farming village on Carnth.|Airen Cracken, ''Cracken's Rebel Field Guide''|<ref name="CRFG" />}}
'''Carnth''' was a [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] located in the [[M'shinni sector/Legends|M'shinni sector]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Imperial Prefect|prefect]] on Carnth promised the [[Farmer/Legends|farmers]] of a local [[Unidentified farming village|village]] substantial rewards for remaining loyal to the Empire, which hampered the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] efforts to establish a small base at the settlement. Eventually, [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] led a Rebel force in an assault on the village that ultimately resulted in the settlement aligning itself with the Alliance.

==Description==
Carnth was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="CRFG" /> located in the [[Carnth system/Legends|Carnth system]], a part of the [[M'shinni sector/Legends|M'shinni sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" />

==History==
{{Quote|The Imperial prefect of the world had made many promises of reward if the farmers remained loyal to the Empire.|Airen Cracken, ''Cracken's Rebel Field Guide''|<ref name="CRFG" />}}
[[File:Airen_Cracken.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Airen Cracken led a Rebel Alliance assault on a farming village on Carnth.]]
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], Carnth was governed by an [[Imperial Prefect|Imperial prefect]]. The Alliance attempted to establish a small base in a [[Unidentified farming village|farming village]] there, but its attempts were subverted by the [[Prefect/Legends|prefect]] promising the [[Farmer/Legends|farmers]] substantial rewards for remaining loyal to the Empire, with the farmers refusing to talk to Rebel representatives.<ref name="CRFG" />

Ultimately, the [[Human/Legends|Human]] Rebel [[Officer/Legends|officer]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] led a force in an assault on the village. After the [[Rebel trooper/Legends|Rebel soldiers]] let loose a volley of bolts from their modified [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] at the farmers charging them, a cease-fire was called, and subsequently intense negotiations with the village elders resulted in the Alliance force being granted entry into the village, with the entire settlement eventually coming to support the Rebellion. Cracken later noted his experiences on Carnth in ''[[Cracken's Rebel Field Guide]]'', a [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] he had [[Author/Legends|composed]] that documented various pieces of equipment used during the Galactic Civil War.<ref name="CRFG" />

==Locations==
{{Quote|The farmers refused to talk to us, but we needed to establish a small base in the strategically located village.|Airen Cracken, ''Cracken's Rebel Field Guide''|<ref name="CRFG" />}}
Carnth was home to a strategically placed village of poorly armed farmers.<ref name="CRFG" />

==Behind the scenes==
Carnth was mentioned in the [[1991]] sourcebook [[Cracken's Rebel Field Guide (sourcebook)|''Cracken's Rebel Field Guide'']], which was written by [[Christopher Kubasik]] and published by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="CRFG" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Carnth [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the world itself, in grid square M-7.<ref name="TEA" />
